name: Floor 1 Phase 2-3 Slice
description: IF DONE documenting, code the game no more creating gdocumentations

goal.answer: Implement and test the Floor 1 end-to-end vertical slice from Phase 1 completion through Phase 2 sequence, Phase 3 final broadcast, and player death — turning the existing note-registration demo into a complete [find words → speak sequence → broadcast monologue → escape or die] loop.

criteria.answer:
`Scripts/World/SequenceManager.cs` exists with `GenerateSequence()`, `ValidateWord()`, `ResetSequence()`, `LockSequence()`, and player-count scaling. Unit tests in `Tests/SequenceManagerTests.cs` pass.
`Scripts/World/RadioItem.cs` exists with 1s pickup delay + 2s grace. `Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs` exists with 10s sustained Tier 2 monologue timer and handoff on broadcaster death.
`GameManager.cs` drives phase state machine Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → Victory, using `SequenceManager.AllWordsRegistered` and `SequenceManager.SequenceComplete` as gates.
`ListenerAI.cs` enters permanent Frenzy when GameManager enters Phase 3 and targets broadcaster/radio location.
Player death sequence in `PlayerController.cs` implements 2s screen fade, last recorded voice playback at reduced pitch, death card UI, and transition to Whisper spectator mode.
`HUDManager.cs` shows sequence display (current/next words) and broadcast progress bar during monologue.
Godot `--headless --quit` smoke test passes with no script/parse errors after all changes.
Manual playtest checklist documented in `.kimchi/ferments/019f0280-44f2-7668-8f0c-b6cf1328fadd/docs/playtest-checklist.md` and completed for the Floor 1 end-to-end loop.

constraints.answer: No new .md documentation files., No new Godot plugins or GDExtensions., No external 3D model/audio downloads., No Steam integration., All new gameplay values must be exported or config-driven (no hardcoded magic numbers in logic)., All cross-system communication via signals/events (no direct UI references from gameplay code)., NUnit tests for pure logic (SequenceManager, WordRegistry).

phases.answer: Sequence Manager + Tests, Radio Item + Broadcast, Phase State Machine + Listener Frenzy, Death Sequence, HUD + Smoke + Playtest

assumptions.answer: Floor 1 Phase 1 (notes, registration, Listener reaction) is already implemented and functional.; `GAME_DESIGN.md` and `design/gdd/game-design-document.md` are the source-of-truth for Phase 2/3/Death mechanics.; Voice tier classification is already implemented in `VoiceManager.cs` and emits a usable TierChanged signal.; Multiplayer synchronization is deferred — this ferment focuses on local/offline behavior first.; Player death voice playback will use a placeholder mechanism since the full Playback Trap recording system is post-launch.; No external art/audio assets will be added; UI is greybox/text-only.; No new Godot plugins or GDExtensions.

phases.name: Sequence Manager + Tests
phases.goal: Implement SequenceManager logic and unit tests for randomized word order, sustained-tier validation, wrong-order reset, and player-count scaling.

steps.description: Create `Scripts/World/SequenceManager.cs`: generate randomized word order from registered words, track current index, validate sustained Tier 2 for 0.5s per word, handle wrong-order reset + 30s lock, implement player-count scaling (sequence length = min(survivors, registeredWords)), emit SequenceRevealed/WordAccepted/WordRejected/SequenceReset/SequenceLocked/SequenceComplete signals.
Create `Tests/SequenceManagerTests.cs` with NUnit tests: sequence generation determinism, wrong-word reset, lock timer, scaling with player count. Tests must pass.

verification.command: test -f Tests/SequenceManagerTests.cs && dotnet test --filter SequenceManager 2>&1 | grep -q 'Passed'

phases.summary: Phase 1 complete. Created `Scripts/World/SequenceManager.cs` with GenerateSequence, OnVoiceUpdate, ResetSequence, LockSequence, all six signals, player-count scaling, sustained Tier-2 validation, wrong-word reset+lock, and lock timer decay. Created `Tests/SequenceManagerTests.cs` with 8 NUnit tests and a testable subclass suppressing Godot signals. Updated csproj with NUnit test packages.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Radio Item + Broadcast
phases.goal: Implement radio item pickup and broadcast system for the Phase 3 monologue.

steps.description: Create `Scripts/World/RadioItem.cs`: Node3D with [Export] pickup delay 1s and grace 2s, Area3D interaction trigger, RadioPickedUp signal, radio mesh placeholder.

Create `Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs`: track broadcaster, 10s monologue timer, sustained Tier 2 requirement, handoff on broadcaster death (reset timer, no progress memory), emit BroadcastStarted/BroadcastProgress/BroadcastComplete/BroadcastFailed.

result.stdout: grep -q 'class RadioBroadcast' Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs && grep -q 'BroadcastStarted' Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs && grep -q 'BroadcastComplete' Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs && grep -q 'StartBroadcast' Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs && grep -q 'OnVoiceUpdate' Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs && grep -q 'OnBroadcasterDied' Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs

phases.summary: Phase 2 complete. Created `Scripts/World/RadioItem.cs` with hold-to-charge pickup (1s delay, 2s grace), Area3D trigger, RadioPickedUp signal, and default mesh placeholder. Created `Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs` with 10s monologue timer, sustained Tier 2 requirement with 2s grace, broadcaster death handoff (reset timer, no progress memory), and four signals: BroadcastStarted, BroadcastProgress, BroadcastComplete, BroadcastFailed.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Phase State Machine + Listener Frenzy
phases.goal: Wire GameManager phase state machine and Listener permanent Frenzy for Phase 3.

steps.description: Extend `GameManager.cs` with a phase state machine: Phase1 → Phase2 (on AllWordsRegistered), Phase2 → Phase3 (on SequenceComplete), Phase3 → Victory (on BroadcastComplete), plus fail states. Spawn RadioItem at appropriate marker when Phase 3 begins.

Extend `ListenerAI.cs` to enter permanent Frenzy when GameManager enters Phase 3. Target broadcaster/Token holder; if no Token, target radio location. Hook into existing Frenzy state without destroying standard 12s Frenzy logic.

phases.summary: Phase 3 complete. GameManager phase state machine is wired with Phase1 → Phase2 → Phase3 → Victory flow and emits Phase3Started when entering Phase 3. ListenerAI subscribes to Phase3Started, sets its permanent-frenzy flag, and handles the new Phase3PermanentFrenzy target mode at top priority: alive Token holder → spawned RadioItem → idle log. Standard 12-second Scream Frenzy logic remains untouched.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Death Sequence
phases.goal: Implement the player catch and death sequence with fade, death card, and spectator transition.

steps.description: Extend `PlayerController.cs::KillByListener()`: add 2s screen fade (CanvasModulate or ColorRect), then show death card UI, transition to Whisper spectator mode (disable movement/collision, enable freecam or fixed spectator view).
Add placeholder last-voice playback: on death, play a stored AudioStream or generated tone representing the player's last recorded voice (since full Playback Trap is post-launch).

phases.name: HUD + Smoke + Playtest
phases.goal: Wire HUD updates, run Godot smoke test, and complete manual playtest checklist.

steps.description: Extend `HUDManager.cs`: add sequence display (current word + remaining words) and broadcast progress bar. Subscribe to SequenceManager and RadioBroadcast signals. Update PhaseTracker to show Phase 2/3 active.
Run Godot --headless smoke test: project parses and compiles without fatal errors.

Create `.kimchi/ferments/019f0280-44f2-7668-8f0c-b6cf1328fadd/docs/playtest-checklist.md` and complete a manual playtest of the Floor 1 loop: pick up note → register word ×4 → sequence reveal → speak sequence → radio pickup → broadcast → win OR die.

goal: Implement and test the Floor 1 end-to-end vertical slice from Phase 1 completion through Phase 2 sequence, Phase 3 final broadcast, and player death — turning the existing note-registration demo into a complete [find words → speak sequence → broadcast monologue → escape or die] loop.
successCriteria: `Scripts/World/SequenceManager.cs` exists with `GenerateSequence()`, `ValidateWord()`, `ResetSequence()`, `LockSequence()`, and player-count scaling. Unit tests in `Tests/SequenceManagerTests.cs` pass.
`Scripts/World/RadioItem.cs` exists with 1s pickup delay + 2s grace. `Scripts/World/RadioBroadcast.cs` exists with 10s sustained Tier 2 monologue timer and handoff on broadcaster death.
`GameManager.cs` drives phase state machine Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → Victory, using `SequenceManager.AllWordsRegistered` and `SequenceManager.SequenceComplete` as gates.
`ListenerAI.cs` enters permanent Frenzy when GameManager enters Phase 3 and targets broadcaster/radio location.
Player death sequence in `PlayerController.cs` implements 2s screen fade, last recorded voice playback at reduced pitch, death card UI, and transition to Whisper spectator mode.
`HUDManager.cs` shows sequence display (current/next words) and broadcast progress bar during monologue.
Godot `--headless --quit` smoke test passes with no script/parse errors after all changes.
Manual playtest checklist documented in `.kimchi/ferments/019f0280-44f2-7668-8f0c-b6cf1328fadd/docs/playtest-checklist.md` and completed for the Floor 1 end-to-end loop.
constraints: No new Godot plugins or GDExtensions.
All new gameplay values must be exported or config-driven (no hardcoded magic numbers in logic).
All cross-system communication via signals/events (no direct UI references from gameplay code).
NUnit tests for pure logic (SequenceManager, WordRegistry).
